summary: A medieval-style fortress with a U-shaped or castle-like layout featuring two tall corner towers connected by a central keep structure. The building is constructed primarily from dark stonebrick with corrugated metal (iron) roofing. Multiple small windows are distributed across the walls, and the structure sits on a stone foundation with a dirt courtyard.

shape.overall_form: U-shaped fortress with two prominent corner towers and central connecting structure
shape.symmetry: Bilateral symmetry with two matching towers flanking a central keep

rebuild_hints: Build two tall rectangular towers (approximately 4 blocks wide, 20 blocks tall) at opposite corners
Connect towers with a central keep structure approximately 10 blocks wide
Create U-shaped footprint with open courtyard facing forward
Add small window openings in a regular grid pattern across all walls (approximately 2x2 block windows spaced 3-4 blocks apart)
Cap all roofs with dark iron or stonebrick slabs creating flat platforms
Add crenellations or stepped edges along roof perimeters
Place stone slab foundation blocks around the base perimeter
Leave central courtyard area open with dark floor material
Ensure towers are taller than central keep by 2-3 blocks for visual hierarchy
uncertainties: Exact interior layout and floor divisions not visible from exterior views
Precise window frame material (appears to be dark wood or iron but not definitively clear)
Whether roof edges have full crenellations or partial stepped design
Exact depth of central keep structure relative to towers
Presence and location of entrance doors (not clearly visible in any view)
